item.title: Pokemon Pokopia Announces New Event With Exclusive Rewards for April 2026
item.description:
This article was updated by Andrea Trama on April 26, 2026, with an explanation of how to repeat the event even after it's gone. It was first published on April 15.

Pokemon Pokopia has announced its third-ever special event, Bulbasaur's Jump Rope Contest, which will give players a chance to snag some fun rewards. The special one-week event in Pokemon Pokopia is scheduled to run from April 19-26, but just like with the first major event, there is an easy way to access it early.

The success of Pokemon Pokopia has been overwhelming. The Pokemon franchise's first-ever life sim sold more than 2 million copies in its first four days on the market, and critical reactions have been great as well, placing it as the highest-rated Pokemon game ever made, just ahead of Pokemon Y . Not resting on its laurels, the development teams at Game Freak and Omega Force have been continuing to add fun activities through special limited-time events, with many more planned for the future.

Pokemon Pokopia Bulbasaur's Jump Rope Contest Dates and Times

  • April 19, 2026, at 5:00 a.m. though April 26, 2026, at 4:59 a.m. (Local time)

The upcoming Pokemon Pokopia event starring the Kanto Grass-type starter will allow players to meet up with Bulbasaur outside the Pokemon Center and participate in a jump rope mini-game, racking up prizes for consecutively completing successful jumps. This mini-game can be tackled alone or in multiplayer, but some special rules will apply. When playing together on a player's primary island, only the host player will receive items, but the player completing the most jumps in one session will hold bragging rights for that world's high score. That doesn't hold true for Cloud Islands, where anyone in a multiplayer jump rope match will be able to both claim the high score, and only the winner of each contest will get the rewards.

If you want to access the event, but you're past April 26 at 5 AM, then you can do this:

  • Go to your Switch 2's home screen (you can simply press the home button, even while in game)
  • Go to System Settings
  • Scroll down to System
  • Within System, scroll down to Date and Time and click on it
  • Click on "Synchronize Clock Using the Internet" if this option is active in order to deactivate it
  • Go to Current Date and Time
  • Set the date to April 19 and the time to anything after 5 AM. The same works for any other day, from April 20 to April 25
  • Launch Pokopia

Shortly after the release of Pokemon Pokopia on March 5, the More Spores for Hoppip event was launched, letting players collect cotton spores and trade them in at the Pokemon Center for picnic-themed prizes, some of which attracted Hoppip and its evolutions to the island. The first in-game event in Pokemon Pokopia lasted for 15 days, and the quickened pace and smaller scale of the Bulbasaur's Jump Rope Contest event means players will have less than half of that time to participate. Also, like the first event, there is a prerequisite to get it started, as players will need to have completed the Yawn Up a Storm request for Slowpoke, which is found in the Withered Wasteland.

Some players were already able to discover the Bulbasaur's Jump Rope Contest event's existence by using time travel methods in Pokemon Pokopia , setting the Switch 2's onboard clock to future dates to see what the game had in store for them. Although the development team hasn't provided a full list of rewards that players can rack up, those who have managed to jump into the jump rope event early have reported that new rewards are available after completing the first jump and every 10 jumps thereafter. Prizes that have been reported include Leppa Berries, Honey, Star Pieces, Large Lost Relics, Rainbow Feathers, Silver Feathers, Pokemetal, and Rare Pokemetal. Additionally, players who are able to complete 51 consecutive jumps will reportedly be rewarded with a special Jump Rope Trophy.

New Questions Added!

GameRant Quiz

—

QUESTION 1/1

Results

0

—

More Games
New Questions Added!
GameRant Quiz
Easy (15s)Medium (10s)Hard (5s)Permadeath (5s)
—
QUESTION 1/1
Results
0
—
More Games

Those rewards may not seem as appealing as the ones in past events, but there is another major Pokemon Pokopia event planned for the end of April. Between April 29 and May 13, players will be able to encounter Sableye and collect exclusive rewards that will allow them to build a suitable habitat for the Darkness Pokemon. Just like with other events, players with a lack of patience don't have to wait for this event to officially start and can use the previously mentioned time travel methods to experience it immediately.

9 /10

Pokemon Pokopia

Follow
Followed

item.title: New Automation Game on Steam Feels Like Satisfactory If You Worked for Helldivers 2’s Super Earth High Command
item.description:
This article was updated by Andrea Trama on April 26, 2026, with a list of games like Helldivers 2 and Satisfactory. It was first published on April 16.

Helldivers 2 took the gaming community by storm for a wide range of reasons. Obviously, its chaotic co-op gameplay and the explosive battles against the Illuminate, Teriminids, and Automatons played a part, as have the galactic map, attractive $40 price tag, and consistent updates. Yet it’s the game’s humor that arguably played the biggest role in its success, as the fight for democracy across outer space resulted in some brilliant marketing and even more brilliant memes. Now, a Steam game called Pax Autocratica is aiming to recapture that kind of magic while also looping in elements of the beloved automation game Satisfactory .

Satisfactory has all the automation staples down, but it goes a step further than connecting pipes and building factories. In addition to a hugely satisfying take on the core mechanics of the genre, elevated by some great sound design and stunning graphics, there’s an impressive open-world to explore with some rewarding exploration. Having that something extra is what truly sets Satisfactory apart, which, in addition to borrowing the first-person perspective, Steam’s Pax Autocratica is striving to do via its roguelike FPS battles.

Per the Pax Autocratica Steam page, the game is set to release on May 8, 2026.

Pax Autocratica Features at a Glance

  • ENJOY a combination of genres, with simulation, base-building, FPS, and roguelite staples.
  • BUILD a colony, MANAGE survivors, LEARN new upgrades through research, and streamline your empire.
  • FIGHT in battles you cause through your rule, experiencing bouts of tactical FPS gameplay as you strive to sway battles in your favor.
  • CHOOSE a benevolent or tyrannical approach to leadership, with inspiration and fear-driven methods to rule.
  • UPGRADE your colony with hundreds of potential buffs per expedition.
  • GATHER resources and TRADE with wandering NPCs.

Games to play if you like Helldivers 2:

  • Deep Rock Galactic
  • Starship Troopers: Extermination
  • Earth Defense Force 5
  • Forever Winter
  • Star Wars Battlefront 2

Games to play if you like Satisfactory:

  • Planet Crafter
  • Factorio
  • Astroneer
  • Factory Town
  • Shapez 2

…shape a grand colony where every life bends to your will. Feel the weight of absolute power as you decree laws, then lead your squads in roguelite FPS battles from brutal skirmishes to large-scale battlefields and unite the Tyris System under your banner.

Guess the games from the emojis.

Gamoji

Guess the game from the emojis.

Time Left: 00:00

Title

—

Clues

—

Results

0 POINTS
—
More Games

Answers

Title

—

—

Clues

—

—

Guess the games from the emojis.
Easy (120s)Medium (90s)Hard (60s)

Pax Autocratica Embraces Helldivers’ Style of Humor, But With Satisfactory’s Depth

Helldivers isn’t exactly subtle with its parody of an overly patriotic way of thinking, and neither is Pax Autocratica with its own critique of monarchies. The game is a “darkly satirical” take on totalitarianism, with players able to choose between being a leader that genuinely seeks the best for their citizens or a cruel monster that seeks nothing but more land to lord over. In addition to Pax Autocratica poking fun at the dictators of the world, its developer, Multiverse, is doing something to impact those affected by conflicts in the real-world. From the moment of release onward, 1% of all revenue from the game will be donated to the Norwegian Refugee Council, a politically neutral group that provides “life-saving assistance” to anyone displaced by worldwide conflict.

While a great cause gives potential players an extra incentive to purchase the game, its actual mechanics seem to be extremely promising as well. Gamers have full control over their state apparatus, from its buildings to its population. They’ll start with a handful of shelters and eventually build massive buildings to house an expanding population, with the citizens in the area commanded to serve a purpose. Games like Satisfactory see players automating machinery, but in Pax Autocratica , they’re automating an entire society. Players will be able to research numerous upgrades, building types, and technology types, and they can use them to make things run more smoothly. If the citizens are properly motivated and carrying out their jobs, efficiency can be boosted drastically, though players will have to choose how they deliver that motivation.

While the goal is to create a hardworking, successful society, the path to that point is yours to forge in whatever way you see fit. Citizens have four traits to manage — Fear, Happiness, Hunger, and Loyalty — and through these traits you can shape your society. Some players will want to take the approach of a benevolent leader, and can do so via comfort, healthcare, and comfortable living areas. Others will want to be the most evil dictator that can be, and Pax Autocratica pulls no punches in this regard. Players can whip workers to make them mine faster, order public executions, place surveillance towers around their colony, and use propaganda to indoctrinate citizens into believing all is well. This kind of freedom would make for a fun colony sim and automation game, but there are also roguelike FPS elements to consider.

Pax Autocratica is Also Worth a Look For Fans of Shooters

As players grow their empire (named Auroria), they’ll eventually have to conquer more land in the fractured territories of the Tyris System. If they do so, they’ll find important citizens to expand their reach, new resources to acquire more upgrades, and trade opportunities. However, if players want to get and keep this land, they’ll have to do so through battle. Succeeding at these conflicts will unlock advanced class skills for the leader and the army, but instead of watching these conflicts play out from a top-down perspective like one might expect, they actually directly involve the player via FPS gameplay.

Subscribe for Pax Autocratica insights and indie coverage

Get deeper context by subscribing to our newsletter: informed breakdowns of Pax Autocratica's roguelike-FPS and colony-sim blend, developer moves, and what it means for fans of Helldivers and Satisfactory—plus similar indie picks.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

In the clips shared for this promising indie game, players will wield grenades, futuristic assault and sniper rifles, and more against ground forces, mechs, and tanks. Not every battle will include the latter two enemy types — some territories players fight for will be held by weaker factions — but it will take a lot of effort to come out of larger conflicts on top. For players who need something more to do and struggle to immerse themselves in pure automation games, this aspect could be game-changing. Pax Autocratica ’s genre blend is hitting a lot of different target audiences, and if everything comes together to create a smooth gameplay loop, it could be a massive hit.

Those interested in Pax Autocratica , but unsure if they want to add it to their Steam wishlists yet, will be glad to know that a demo can be downloaded for the game right now.

Drag weapons to fill the grid

Loadout Logic

Tap on a weapon to rotate it or press R while dragging.

Time —
Filled 0/36

Results

0
—
More Games
Drag weapons to fill the grid
EasyMediumHard

Loadout Logic

Tap on a weapon to rotate it or press R while dragging.
Time —
Filled 0/36
Results
0
—
More Games

Obviously, Helldivers 2 fans who love the game primarily for its co-op may want to find a different Helldivers alternative. Sure, the humor and tone are there, as is the sci-fi setting and weaponry, but it is fully singleplayer. Still, for those willing to try something a bit different, Pax Automatica seems to have a ton of depth and unique elements to its name. It certainly seems immersive on paper, and players won’t have to wait long to see if everything melds together nicely since this hodgepodge of genres comes out in May.

item.title: How to Quit Vampire Crawlers
item.description:
Vampire Crawlers is a game that players won't have any problems getting sucked into. Even if their initial plan was to play a run and then leave, it won't be difficult for them to immediately jump onto the next one, and before they know it, a couple of hours will have passed.

However, eventually, the time will come when they will have to shut off their game. Unfortunately, it's not as straightforward as it sounds. Players will have to go through a few menus in order to quit Vampire Crawlers . Thankfully, this guide tells them how it's done in case they haven't found the option to do it yet.

How to Quit Vampire Crawlers

It is likely that gamers will have problems finding the option to quit Vampire Crawlers , as it is not available with the simple click of a button. To quit Vampire Crawlers , players have to open Settings, which is represented by a Graveyard in the village, and then select Exit to Menu . There are quite a few locations in the village, so players might not see the Graveyard right away; it is located between the Town Hall (Unlocks) and the Inn (Crawlers). If players are mid-run, they can hit pause and then select Exit to Menu .

Exiting to menu will take players to the main menu, and here, they can finally exit the game by clicking the Quit button .

Subscribe to the newsletter for Vampire Crawlers help

Get step-by-step Vampire Crawlers help—subscribe to the newsletter for clear how-tos on quitting runs, locating menu options like the Graveyard, and reliable in-game procedures to avoid lost progress.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

Players who want to quickly leave the game without having to go through these menus can press Alt + F4 simultaneously on PC or close the app on their console instead. However, they must be sure to do this after finishing a battle so they don't lose their progress.

Vampire Crawlers: The Turbo Wildcard from Vampire Survivors

item.title: Rumor: Delicious in Dungeon Game in Development
item.description:
Popular anime Delicious in Dungeon could be getting a video game adaptation. Originally created by manga artist Ryoko Kui, Delicious in Dungeon , also known as Dungeon Meshi , takes a lot of inspiration from Dungeons and Dragons , and fans often compare it to various RPGs due to the story's themes.

The first season of Delicious in Dungeon anime premiered in 2024, which ran for 24 episodes. The series was received incredibly well by fans worldwide as it combined high fantasy themes with cooking and humor. It follows a party consisting of Laios the warrior, Marcille the elf mage, Senshi the dwarf, and Chilchuck the half-foot locksmith, as they fight through various dungeons to defeat a dragon that ate his sister, Falin. Along the way, Laios and the gang discover creative ways to cook and eat monsters in order to keep their energy up throughout their journey. The cooking parts added an endearing aspect to the usual dungeon-crawling plot, which contributed to Delicious in Dungeon 's massive success.

Delicious in Dungeon Game Could Finally Be in Development

Delicious in Dungeon creator Ryoko Kui has always been open about her love for fantasy media, especially story-rich RPGs like the Baldur's Gate and Pathfinder franchises. And with the anime's popularity, it's natural for fans to hope for a video game inspired by it, especially with equally huge titles like Demon Slayer getting multiple games. It seems like the dream might turn into a reality because Reddit user capitalggamer1 reported in a post that a Delicious in Dungeon game is allegedly being developed by Japan-based studio Toydea.

They shared a series of screenshots of early builds from 2023 — which would mean that it's supposedly in development before the first season premiered — showing the character models for Laios, Marcille, Senshi, and Chilchuck, including windows showing their stats, abilities, and equipment. One screenshot also shows them exploring a dungeon, which is reminiscent of Baldur's Gate 's gameplay. The release date for the rumored game wasn't disclosed and Toydea has yet to confirm the news, so fans should take the information and the early builds with a grain of salt for now.

In the comments, many Delicious in Dungeon fans expressed their excitement for the rumored video game. They also stated that Ryoko Kui would love the idea of her creation being turned into a video game considering her affinity for RPGs. Meanwhile, some asked if Toydea's Delicious in Dungeon game would have a cooking mechanic as well, since that's the story's central theme and what made the manga and anime stand out. The screenshots provided by capitalggamer1 didn't show any cooking, but it's still possible to have a cooking feature that's similar to various RPGs and JRPGs like Monster Hunter World and Final Fantasy 15 .

Guess the games from the emojis.

Gamoji

Guess the game from the emojis.

Time Left: 00:00

Title

—

Clues

—

Results

0 POINTS

Answers
Title
—
—
Clues
—
—

One could argue that it might be too early to come out with a Delicious in Dungeon game since the anime only has one season, and it's still unclear when Season 2 will officially premiere. But the first season alone could provide so much content for the rumored game, and Toydea could also have creative liberty for how the story will go, especially if it's going to be choice-driven like other Dungeons and Dragons -inspired video games. At the same time, it would be a great opportunity to come out with multiple games that cover one or two seasons of the anime, just like what Demon Slayer did with Hinokami Chronicles .

item.title: Rumor: Star Fox Could Be Getting 2 New Games, Insider Claims
item.description:
A report from an industry insider claims that Nintendo may be working on multiple new Star Fox games. The rumor comes amid a big year for the Star Fox franchise, which saw the characters thrust back into the spotlight in 2026, marking the first time in years that Nintendo seems focused on the series.

Nintendo has a history of allowing some of its IP to remain dormant for years, including games like Star Fox. Although series star Fox McCloud and his crew have seen a handful of games released over the last few generations, such as Star Fox Zero, the franchise's more recent entries have not been as warmly received as titles like Star Fox 64 or the Nintendo GameCube's Star Fox Adventures. With Nintendo's recent push to create movies around its franchises, the possibility of a resurgence of the series feels more likely than ever.

Star Fox May Be Getting a Multiplayer Spin-off as Well as a New Single-Player Game

The Star Fox franchise could see the release of multiple new games, with a multiplayer spin-off coming in 2026 and a single-player-focused title releasing in 2027, according to new rumors from industry insiders. The claim comes from a ResetEra thread where users discussed comments made by Oscar Lemaire, a respected reporter from French gaming website Gamekult. Posts from the ResetEra thread state that Lemaire reported on two Star Fox games being worked on, with one focused on multiplayer, although it should be noted that commenters from a Reddit thread on the topic suggest that this information could be inaccurate. Reddit users claiming to have tuned in to Lemaire's live stream, where the industry veteran first discussed the rumor, have argued that he merely implied that the upcoming Star Fox game would also include a multiplayer mode.

Rumors of a new Star Fox title have picked up in recent months, stemming around the franchise's well-publicized role in The Super Mario Galaxy Movie. Insiders have claimed that the game will have a stronger narrative focus than Star Fox Zero, which launched in 2016 for the Wii U. In that title, communication and story beats between Fox McCloud and company were limited to comms broadcasts that occurred mid-mission, cutting down on the cutscenes and larger story beats that fans may be hoping for this time around.

With Fox's appearance in the latest Mario movie, long-time series fans are hoping that the Star Fox franchise is in for a comeback. After a hot streak that began with the Super Nintendo's original Star Fox titles and tapered out after the cult classic Star Fox Adventures, Nintendo players are long overdue for a brand-new reason to be excited about the series. The Nintendo Switch 2 is the perfect platform for such a grand return, with a fresh library that's currently in need of new first-party Nintendo titles.

Find all 10 pairs

Results

0
—
More Games
Find all 10 pairs
Regular Cover ArtClean Cover Art
Results
0
—
More Games

If the recent rumors about upcoming Nintendo Switch 2 titles prove to be true, Nintendo is in for a major year in 2026. Star Fox could help begin the surge of titles, helping to end what many consider to be a slow period regarding big Nintendo releases. Series fans should stay tuned for any news of what may be a major return to form for the Star Fox crew.

8 /10

Star Fox Zero
